turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Hi, thanks for coming in today. I'm Alex, the hiring manager for the Senior Software Engineer position. Please, have a seat. Can you start by telling me a bit about yourself and your relevant experience?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Uh, sure, hi Alex. I'm Jordan, nice to meet you. So, I've been in software development for about eight years now, mostly full-stack work with React and Node.js. Last role at TechNova, I led a team building a customer dashboard that handled, like, millions of data points daily. Really proud of that.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: That sounds impressive. TechNova—can you remind me how long you were there and what your exact role was?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Yeah, I was there for four years, from 2019 to 2023. Senior Full-Stack Developer, reporting directly to the CTO. We scaled the app from scratch, integrated AWS services, the whole nine yards.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Great. On your resume, you mentioned leading that dashboard project. Walk me through the tech stack and any challenges you faced.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Right, so frontend was React with Redux for state management, backend Node.js and Express, database was PostgreSQL with some Redis caching. Biggest challenge was, um, real-time updates— we used Socket.io to fix latency issues. Deployed it all on AWS, yeah.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Socket.io on AWS, nice. But earlier you said Node.js—did you have any experience with Python or Django in that role? Your resume lists Python under skills, but not tied to TechNova.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Oh, Python? Uh, yeah, we dabbled a bit in some microservices, but mostly Node. Python was more from my previous gig at StartUpX, I think. Wait, no—actually, at TechNova we prototyped a data pipeline with Django once, but it didn't go to prod.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Interesting. Let's check your resume again. For StartUpX, you have it as 2017-2019, Junior Developer, and no mention of leading any projects there. But now you're saying Python was primarily there? And at TechNova, you led the team—how many people were on that team?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Team size? Um, it was small, like three devs including me, and a designer. Yeah, and Python—honestly, I might've mixed it up. StartUpX was more vanilla JS and PHP, actually. TechNova is where React came in heavy.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: PHP at StartUpX? Your resume doesn't list PHP at all, and it emphasizes React/Node from the start of your career. Also, leading a team of three including yourself—did you do any hiring or performance reviews?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Hiring? Nah, not formally. I mean, I onboarded the designer and mentored the junior dev a bit. Look, it was a startup vibe, you know? Everyone wore multiple hats. But yeah, the dashboard was my baby—I architected the whole thing.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: I see. One more thing: you claimed eight years experience. StartUpX two years, TechNova four—that's six. What about before StartUpX? Resume shows a gap or freelance?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Oh, right, freelance and some college projects. Two years of contract work, building e-commerce sites with, uh, WordPress and custom JS. Counts as experience, totally. It all adds up.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: WordPress for e-commerce? Okay, thanks for clarifying. We'll review everything and be in touch soon.

input.objective: Job interview for Senior Software Engineer where candidate contradicts resume claims about experience and tech stack
